destructive.name: aws-secretsmanager-put-secret-value
destructive.reason: aws secretsmanager put-secret-value creates a new secret version and can break clients.

destructive.name: aws-ssm-delete-parameters

destructive.reason: doppler projects delete removes a project.

destructive.name: doppler-environments-delete

destructive.name: doppler-configs-delete

destructive.name: op-item-delete

destructive.name: op-document-delete
destructive.reason: op document delete removes secure documents (including archive operations).

destructive.name: op-vault-delete

destructive.name: op-user-delete

destructive.name: op-group-delete

destructive.name: op-connect-token-delete

destructive.name: vault-secrets-disable

destructive.name: vault-kv-destroy
destructive.reason: vault kv destroy permanently deletes secret versions.

destructive.name: vault-kv-metadata-delete
destructive.reason: vault kv metadata delete removes all versions and metadata for a secret.

destructive.name: vault-kv-delete
destructive.reason: vault kv delete removes the latest secret version.

destructive.name: vault-delete
destructive.reason: vault delete removes secrets at a path.

destructive.name: vault-policy-delete
destructive.reason: vault policy delete removes access policies.

destructive.name: vault-auth-disable
destructive.reason: vault auth disable disables an auth method.

destructive.name: vault-token-revoke
destructive.reason: vault token revoke invalidates tokens and can disrupt access.

destructive.name: vault-lease-revoke
destructive.reason: vault lease revoke invalidates leases and can disrupt access.

destructive.name: az-storage-container-delete
destructive.reason: az storage container delete removes an Azure storage container.

destructive.name: az-storage-blob-delete-batch
destructive.reason: az storage blob delete-batch removes multiple blobs from Azure storage.

destructive.name: az-storage-blob-delete
destructive.reason: az storage blob delete removes a blob from Azure storage.

destructive.name: az-storage-account-delete
destructive.reason: az storage account delete removes an entire Azure storage account.

destructive.name: azcopy-remove
destructive.reason: azcopy remove deletes files from Azure storage.

destructive.name: azcopy-sync-delete
destructive.reason: azcopy sync --delete-destination removes destination files not in source.

destructive.name: gsutil-rb
destructive.reason: gsutil rb removes a GCS bucket.

destructive.name: gsutil-rm
destructive.reason: gsutil rm deletes objects from GCS.

destructive.name: gsutil-rsync-delete
destructive.reason: gsutil rsync -d deletes destination objects not in source.

destructive.name: gcloud-storage-buckets-delete
destructive.reason: gcloud storage buckets delete removes a GCS bucket.

destructive.name: gcloud-storage-objects-delete
destructive.reason: gcloud storage objects delete removes objects from GCS.

destructive.name: gcloud-storage-rm
destructive.reason: gcloud storage rm removes objects from GCS.

destructive.name: mc-rb
destructive.reason: mc rb removes a MinIO bucket.

destructive.name: mc-rm
destructive.reason: mc rm deletes objects from MinIO.

destructive.name: mc-admin-bucket-delete
destructive.reason: mc admin bucket delete removes a bucket via admin API.

destructive.name: mc-mirror-remove
destructive.reason: mc mirror --remove deletes destination objects not in source.

destructive.name: mc-admin-user-remove
destructive.reason: mc admin user remove/disable affects user access.

destructive.name: mc-admin-policy-remove
destructive.reason: mc admin policy remove/unset modifies access policies.

destructive.name: s3-rb
destructive.reason: aws s3 rb removes an S3 bucket and is destructive.

destructive.name: s3-rm
destructive.reason: aws s3 rm deletes S3 objects and is destructive.

destructive.name: s3-sync-delete
destructive.reason: aws s3 sync --delete removes destination objects not in source.

destructive.name: s3api-delete-bucket
destructive.reason: aws s3api delete-bucket permanently deletes a bucket.

destructive.name: s3api-delete-object
destructive.reason: aws s3api delete-object permanently deletes an object.

destructive.name: s3api-delete-objects
destructive.reason: aws s3api delete-objects permanently deletes multiple objects.

destructive.name: push-force-any
destructive.reason: Force push (even with --force-with-lease) can rewrite remote history. Disabled in strict mode.

destructive.name: rebase
destructive.reason: git rebase rewrites commit history. Disabled in strict mode.

destructive.name: commit-amend
destructive.reason: git commit --amend rewrites the last commit. Disabled in strict mode.

destructive.name: cherry-pick
destructive.reason: git cherry-pick can introduce duplicate commits. Review carefully.

destructive.name: filter-branch
destructive.reason: git filter-branch rewrites entire repository history. Extremely dangerous!

destructive.name: filter-repo
destructive.reason: git filter-repo rewrites repository history. Review carefully.

destructive.name: reflog-expire
destructive.reason: git reflog expire removes reflog entries needed for recovery.

destructive.name: gc-aggressive
destructive.reason: git gc with aggressive/prune options can remove recoverable objects.

destructive.name: worktree-remove
destructive.reason: git worktree remove deletes a linked working tree.

destructive.name: submodule-deinit
destructive.reason: git submodule deinit removes submodule configuration.

destructive.name: add-all-dot
destructive.reason: git add . stages everything including secrets, .env files, and build artifacts. Use 'git add <specific-files>' instead.

destructive.name: add-all-flag
destructive.reason: git add -A/--all stages all changes including secrets, .env files, and build artifacts. Use 'git add <specific-files>' instead.

destructive.name: push-master
destructive.reason: Direct push to master is blocked. Use a feature branch and open a Pull Request.

destructive.name: push-main
destructive.reason: Direct push to main is blocked. Use a feature branch and open a Pull Request.

destructive.name: dd-device
destructive.reason: dd to a block device will OVERWRITE all data on that device. Extremely dangerous!

destructive.name: dd-wipe
destructive.reason: dd from /dev/zero or /dev/urandom to a device will WIPE all data!

destructive.name: fdisk-edit
destructive.reason: fdisk can modify partition tables and cause data loss.

destructive.name: parted-modify
destructive.reason: parted can modify partition tables and cause data loss.

destructive.name: mkfs
destructive.reason: mkfs formats a partition/device and ERASES all existing data.

destructive.name: mkswap
destructive.reason: mkswap formats a partition as a swap area, ERASING any existing data.

destructive.name: wipefs
destructive.reason: wipefs removes filesystem signatures. Use with extreme caution.

destructive.name: mount-bind-root
destructive.reason: mount --bind to root directory can have system-wide effects.

destructive.name: umount-force
destructive.reason: umount -f force unmounts which may cause data loss if device is in use.

destructive.name: losetup-device
destructive.reason: losetup modifies loop device associations. Verify before proceeding.

destructive.name: mdadm-stop
destructive.reason: mdadm --stop shuts down a RAID array. Data may become inaccessible.

destructive.name: mdadm-remove
destructive.reason: mdadm --remove removes a drive from a RAID array. May cause data loss if redundancy is lost.

destructive.name: mdadm-fail
destructive.reason: mdadm --fail marks a device as failed. Use only for intentional drive replacement.

destructive.name: mdadm-zero-superblock
destructive.reason: mdadm --zero-superblock PERMANENTLY erases RAID metadata. Array cannot be reassembled.

destructive.name: mdadm-create
destructive.reason: mdadm --create initializes a new RAID array, ERASING existing data on member devices.

destructive.name: mdadm-grow
destructive.reason: mdadm --grow reshapes a RAID array. Interruption can cause data loss. Backup first.

destructive.name: btrfs-subvolume-delete
destructive.reason: btrfs subvolume delete PERMANENTLY removes a subvolume and all its data.

destructive.name: btrfs-device-remove
destructive.reason: btrfs device remove redistributes data off a device. Interruption causes data loss.

destructive.name: btrfs-device-add
destructive.reason: btrfs device add incorporates a device into the filesystem. Verify the device is correct.

destructive.name: btrfs-balance
destructive.reason: btrfs balance redistributes data across devices. Can be slow and disruptive.

destructive.name: btrfs-check-repair
destructive.reason: btrfs check --repair is DANGEROUS and can cause data loss. Backup first!

destructive.name: btrfs-rescue
destructive.reason: btrfs rescue operations modify filesystem metadata. Use only as last resort.

destructive.name: btrfs-filesystem-resize
destructive.reason: btrfs filesystem resize can shrink a filesystem. Data loss if size is too small.

destructive.name: dmsetup-remove
destructive.reason: dmsetup remove detaches a device-mapper device. May cause data loss if in use.

destructive.name: dmsetup-remove-all
destructive.reason: dmsetup remove_all removes ALL device-mapper devices. Extremely dangerous!

destructive.name: dmsetup-wipe-table
destructive.reason: dmsetup wipe_table replaces the device table, causing all I/O to fail.

destructive.name: dmsetup-clear
destructive.reason: dmsetup clear removes the mapping table from a device.

destructive.name: dmsetup-load
destructive.reason: dmsetup load changes device mapping. Verify the new table is correct.

destructive.name: dmsetup-create
destructive.reason: dmsetup create sets up a new device-mapper device. Verify parameters carefully.

destructive.name: nbd-client-disconnect
destructive.reason: nbd-client -d disconnects a network block device. Data loss if not properly unmounted.

destructive.name: nbd-client-connect
destructive.reason: nbd-client connecting a device can expose or overwrite data. Verify server and device.

destructive.name: pvremove
destructive.reason: pvremove ERASES LVM metadata from a physical volume. Data becomes inaccessible.

destructive.name: vgremove
destructive.reason: vgremove DELETES a volume group and all logical volumes within it.

destructive.name: lvremove
destructive.reason: lvremove PERMANENTLY deletes a logical volume and ALL its data.

destructive.name: vgreduce
destructive.reason: vgreduce removes a physical volume from a volume group. Data may be lost.

destructive.name: lvreduce
destructive.reason: lvreduce SHRINKS a logical volume. Data loss if filesystem isn't resized first!

destructive.name: lvresize-shrink
destructive.reason: lvresize with negative size SHRINKS the volume. Resize filesystem first or lose data!

destructive.name: pvmove
destructive.reason: pvmove migrates data between physical volumes. Do NOT interrupt or data may be lost.

destructive.name: lvconvert-merge
destructive.reason: lvconvert --merge reverts LV to snapshot state, discarding changes since snapshot.

destructive.name: chmod-777
destructive.reason: chmod 777 makes files world-writable. This is a security risk.

destructive.name: chmod-recursive-root
destructive.reason: chmod -R on system directories can break system permissions.

destructive.name: chown-recursive-root
destructive.reason: chown -R on system directories can break system ownership.

destructive.name: chmod-setuid
destructive.reason: Setting setuid bit (chmod u+s) is a security-sensitive operation.

destructive.name: chmod-setgid
destructive.reason: Setting setgid bit (chmod g+s) is a security-sensitive operation.

destructive.name: chown-to-root
destructive.reason: Changing ownership to root should be done carefully.

destructive.name: setfacl-all
destructive.reason: setfacl -R on system directories can modify access control across the filesystem.

destructive.name: systemctl-stop-critical
destructive.reason: Stopping/disabling critical services can cause system access loss or outage.

destructive.name: systemctl-stop
destructive.reason: systemctl stop/disable/mask affects service availability. Verify service name.

destructive.name: service-stop-critical
destructive.reason: Stopping critical services can cause system access loss.

destructive.name: systemctl-isolate
destructive.reason: systemctl isolate changes the system state significantly.

destructive.name: systemctl-power
destructive.reason: systemctl poweroff/reboot/halt will shut down or restart the system.

destructive.name: shutdown
destructive.reason: shutdown will power off or restart the system.

destructive.name: reboot
destructive.reason: reboot will restart the system.

destructive.name: init-level
destructive.reason: init 0 shuts down, init 6 reboots the system.
